title: SSO a-la-carte option (addon for normal plans, not enterprise)
excerpt: SSO has become widely accessible and easy for small teams and startups to implement (it is baked in to many of the google/microsoft365 plans at this point). It also significantly increases security and compliance while d…

posts.author: dmurawsky (Derek Murawsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
SSO has become widely accessible and easy for small teams and startups to implement (it is baked in to many of the google/microsoft365 plans at this point). It also significantly increases security and compliance while decreasing administrative burden. As such, I would like to ask for SSO to be available outside of the Enterprise plan. Ideally, it would be a free offering, but it would be reasonable to charge a small amount per month per user in addition to the base fee.

For more information on why SSO should not be an enterprise only feature, please see the following articles:

The SSO Wall of Shame

The SSO Wall of Shame

A list of vendors that treat single sign-on as a luxury feature, not a core security requirement.

Kolide

Explaining the Backlash to the SSO Tax

Nobody likes paying extra for security features. So why isn't the SSO tax going anywhere?
